It is 22 to be one of the Four Great Classical Novels of China. In fact, the masterpiece of Chinese literature brought Chinese fiction to the highest point. It is of such significance 23 a new field of literature research thus generated — “the Redology,” the study specifically devoted to this great work of literature. Many people believe that Hongloumeng 24 the real life of the author. It reflects the rise and decline of Cao’s family as well as the Qing Dynasty. As the author mentions in the first chapter, it is a memorial to the young ladies he knew in his youth, 25 his friends, relatives and servants. The novel is remarkable not only for its detailed portray of a huge cast of characters 26 for its precise observation of the life and social structures of the 18th-century China.

There is a recap available for every game. ▲ 閱讀下文,回答第 34-37 題。 In the predictable future, cash may be replaced by alterative options. People can pay by using credit cards, debit cards, electronic payment apps, or mobile payment services. Without cash, payments are processed electronically. That is, people transfer funds to another person or business instead of using paper currency and coins. Some supporters claim the benefits of the cashless society. First, there will be less crime; illegal deals like money laundering and drug trade will reduce because every cashless transaction leaves a paper record. Furthermore, international payments will become much easier. When the two nations are able to handle cashless transactions, people do not have to buy local currency when going abroad. While a zero-cash world seems appealing, some people are concerned about the disadvantages of it. They worry that personal information will be exposed to a high risk of abuse. Next, technology problems may cause inconvenience to people in accessing their money. Besides, those without bank accounts will have trouble paying and receiving payments. In spite of the warnings and opposition, several nations have taken action to eliminate cash with the push from both the consumers and governments. They believe that with sufficient planning and proper measures to help people of all economic status adjust to the system, it is possible to make cash history.

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 108-1-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: What is the passage mainly about?
options: To show medical students how to perform a surgery on the shoulder.
To offer common ways for middle-aged people to remain healthy.
To explain the causes, symptoms, and treatments of a shoulder problem.
To raise questions about the regulations of producing frozen food.
questions.explanation: 這一段文章主要是關於什麼? (A) 向醫學生展示如何進行肩膀手術。

questions.subject: 英文
questions.unit: 108-1-四技二專-00共同科目 英文

questions.unit: 108-1-四技二專-00共同科目 英文
questions.q: Which of the following has the closest meaning to the word “ diminish ” in paragraph 2?
questions.explanation: 下列何者與第二段的 diminish 一字的字義最接近? 遣詞沒有不當之處。 (A) 慶祝 (B) 道歉 (C) 參與 (D) 減少 能適切表達原文題意,文法、標點、用字遣詞 3

questions.q: According to the passage, which of the following is true?
options: Patients with frozen shoulder usually have a quick recovery within days after the surgery.
The connective tissues around the joint of a frozen shoulder are thicker and tighter.
Most frozen shoulders can only improve with a number of surgical procedures.
During the thawing stage, the patient’s shoulder is too stiff to move a bit.
questions.explanation: 根據文章,下列何者正確? 僅有少數不當之處。 (A) 凝肩的病人通常手術後幾天內就會迅速復原。 僅能部分表達原文題意,文法、標點、用字遣 2 (B) 凝肩的關節附近的結締組織較厚、較緊繃。 詞有多處失當之處。 (C) 多數的凝肩唯有經過一些手術才會改善。 無法表達原文題意,文法、標點、用字遣詞僅 1 (D) 在解凍期,病人的肩膀會僵硬到不能動。 有少數正確之處。 (1) 空白 ( 未書寫文字 ) 。 第二部分:非選擇題 (2) 文字瑣碎、形同未答。 0 I. 填充 (3) 只抄題目或其他試題。
